target.evidence: Both target advanced semiconductor manufacturing and supply chain resilience.

connections.evidence_summary: China Interim Measures for Generative AI Services (effective 15 August 2023) created the mandatory regulatory approval framework; Baidu ERNIE Bot public release on 31 August 2023 was explicitly contingent on obtaining approval under this framework.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Interim Measures for the Administration of Generative AI Services, jointly issued by CAC and six other ministries on 13 July 2023, effective 15 August 2023, required generative AI service providers to complete algorithm filing and obtain regulatory approval before offering public-facing services. ERNIE Bot public release on 31 August 2023 — 16 days after the Measures took effect — followed receipt of approval from the CAC as part of the first batch of approved generative AI services.
evidence_detail.enabling_relationship: The Interim Measures created the regulatory permission framework for public-facing generative AI services; ERNIE Bot exercised those permissions as one of the first batch of approved services. Multi-ministry regulatory framework enabling a single corporate product release.

sources.desc: TechNode — ERNIE Bot public release with regulatory approval context

Lexing Network — ERNIE Bot regulatory compliance analysis

connections.evidence_summary: GenAI Interim Measures establish approval framework for public GenAI services; Doubao (Yunque) among first batch of 8 enterprises approved August 31, 2023

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services (生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法) took effect August 15, 2023. First batch of 8 enterprises approved to provide public GenAI services announced August 31, 2023. ByteDance's Yunque Large Model (云雀大模型, later branded as Doubao/豆包) confirmed among approved entities.

citation_text.chinese: 8月31日消息，百度、字节、商汤、中科院旗下紫东太初、百川智能、智谱华章等8家企业/机构的大模型位列首批通过《生成式人工智能服务管理暂行办法》备案的名单，可正式上线面向公众提供服务。
citation_text.english: On August 31, news emerged that the large models of 8 enterprises/institutions including Baidu, ByteDance, SenseTime, CAS Zidong Taichu, Baichuan Intelligence, and Zhipu AI are on the list of the first batch to pass the filing under the 'Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services' and can officially go online to provide services to the public.

evidence_detail.enabling_relationship: The Interim Measures for Generative AI Services create the regulatory framework enabling public deployment of generative AI services in China. Doubao’s first-batch approval (31 August 2023) demonstrates utilisation of this regulatory pathway.

yunque_doubao_lineage.confirmation: Doubao is an AI product developed by ByteDance based on the Yunque model (which was one of the first batch of large models to pass algorithm filing)

sources.desc: CAC GenAI Interim Measures Official Text

connections.evidence_summary: Both advanced semiconductor export control frameworks; companion SME IFR footnote references Japan's July 2023 equipment controls

mechanism.evidence: Both use export control restrictions on semiconductor technology. Netherlands-Japan-US Semiconductor Controls: trilateral semiconductor manufacturing equipment controls. BIS Advanced Computing IFR: advanced computing IC controls with expanded FDP rule.

target.evidence: Both target advanced semiconductor technology flows, primarily restricting PRC access to controlled items.

scope.evidence: Both comprehensive semiconductor export control frameworks with extraterritorial provisions.

timing.evidence: Netherlands-Japan-US Semiconductor Controls: July 2023. BIS Advanced Computing IFR: November 2023. Gap: ~4 months (within 6-month Security domain window).

evidence_detail.transparency_note: US is a participant in the Netherlands-Japan-US trilateral semiconductor export control coordination. No documentary evidence of the trilateral arrangement is cited in the IFR operative text.
evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Companion Semiconductor Manufacturing Equipment IFR (88 FR 73424) footnote: 'The Government of Japan added ArF-wet lithography equipment and other advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to its control list for all regions on July 23, 2023.' Establishes documentary acknowledgement of parallel action.

connections.evidence_summary: Both international AI standards published within 10 months by different standards bodies (ISO/IEC and IEEE). Parallel standard-setting efforts addressing AI governance foundations.

mechanism.evidence: Both formal international standards publications by major standards bodies (IEEE and ISO/IEC) addressing AI technology governance. Same policy tool: international technical standard publication.

target.evidence: IEEE 7000 addresses ethical AI system design processes. ISO/IEC 22989 establishes foundational AI concepts and terminology. Both target AI technology governance and development ecosystem.

scope.evidence: Both international scope — IEEE and ISO/IEC are global standards bodies. Comparable organisational scale.

timing.evidence: IEEE 7000: 15 September 2021. ISO/IEC 22989: July 2022. Gap: ~10 months. Within 12-month Technology domain window.

connections.evidence_summary: Google Gemma explicitly documented as ‘built from the same research and technology used to create the Gemini models.’ Parent framework documentation identifies both as same model family.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Google AI for Developers documentation states: 'Gemma is a family of lightweight, state-of-the-art open models built from the same research and technology used to create the Gemini models. connections.evidence_summary: Kimi K2 technical report documents MLA attention mechanism derived from DeepSeek-V3 architecture, of which V3.1 is the direct continuation via continued pretraining.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Kimi K2 Technical Report (arXiv 2507.20534v1, Section 2.3 Model Architecture) formally documents that K2 employs multi-head latent attention derived from the DeepSeek-V3 architecture. DeepSeek-V3.1 is the direct continuation of V3 via 840 billion tokens of continued pretraining.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: Kimi K2 adopted the DeepSeek-V3 MLA architecture. V3.1 is the direct V3 continuation, establishing shared architectural lineage across developers.
evidence_detail.citation_text: The model architecture follows an ultra-sparse MoE with multi-head latent attention (MLA) similar to DeepSeek-V3, derived from empirical scaling law analysis.

connections.evidence_summary: Kimi K2 technical report documents MLA attention mechanism derived from DeepSeek-V3 architecture shared by DeepSeek-R1.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Kimi K2 Technical Report (ResearchGate, Section 2.3) documents that K2's architecture follows a design similar to DeepSeek-V3, employing MLA as the attention mechanism. DeepSeek-R1 shares the V3 architectural base, establishing cross-developer family membership.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: Kimi K2 adopted the DeepSeek-V3 MLA architecture. R1 shares the same V3 base, creating a Framework Family connection through common architectural ancestry.
evidence_detail.citation_text: The architecture follows a similar design to DeepSeek-V3, employing Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) as the attention mechanism, with a model hidden dimension of 7168 and an MoE expert hidden dimension of 2048.

connections.evidence_summary: Qwen3-Coder officially designated as the code specialisation of the Qwen3 model family by the developing entity and confirmed by AWS.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: QwenLM Official GitHub pinned repository description designates Qwen3-Coder as 'the code version of Qwen3.' AWS Builder Content independently confirms the model is built upon the Qwen3 series foundational architecture.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: Qwen3-Coder is the code-specialised extension of the Qwen3 model family, with officially documented parent-child architectural relationship.
evidence_detail.citation_text: Qwen3-Coder is the code version of Qwen3, the large language model series developed by Qwen team.

connections.evidence_summary: DeepSeek-V3.1 built via continued pretraining on V3 base architecture shared with DeepSeek-R1, establishing architectural family membership.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: DeepSeek API Documentation release announcement states V3.1 was built via continued pretraining on top of V3. DeepSeek-R1 is also built on the V3 base. Both models are members of the DeepSeek V3 architectural family.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: V3.1 and R1 are sibling models within the DeepSeek architectural family, both derived from the V3 base architecture.
evidence_detail.citation_text: V3.1 Base: 840B tokens continued pretraining for long context extension on top of V3.

connections.evidence_summary: UNGA Resolution 78/311 establishes AI capacity-building mandate (July 1, 2024); UNIDO AIM Global CoE launched 3 days later as 'implementation partner for the new UN Resolution on AI' (DG Müller, WAIC 2024)

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: UNGA Resolution 78/311 'Enhancing international cooperation on capacity-building of artificial intelligence' (adopted July 1, 2024) establishes international cooperation mandate for AI capacity-building in developing countries. UNIDO AIM Global Centre of Excellence (launched July 4, 2024 at WAIC, Shanghai) provides operational implementation mechanism through UN agency infrastructure.

authority_hierarchy.target: UNIDO Alliance for AI and Manufacturing (AIM) Global CoE (UN specialised agency operational implementation)

citation_text.unido_dg: UNIDO stands ready to be an implementation partner for the new UN Resolution on AI to ensure that developing countries, especially LDCs, benefit equally from AI. With our Global Alliance on AI for Industry and Manufacturing, UNIDO offers a bridge between industrialized countries, the private sector, academia and developing countries.
citation_text.unido_ddg: The recent resolution on strengthening international cooperation in AI capacity building, adopted by the 78th United Nations General Assembly on July 1
citation_text.speaker_context: UNIDO Director General Gerd Müller and Deputy Director General Ciyong Zou at WAIC 2024, Shanghai, July 4, 2024

evidence_detail.implementation_mapping: Resolution 78/311 OP1 resolves to 'bridge the artificial intelligence and other digital divides' and 'enhance international cooperation on capacity-building in developing countries.' UNIDO AIM operationalises this through: (1) bridging industrialised and developing countries via institutional partnership infrastructure, (2) private sector and academia engagement mechanisms, (3) focus on LDCs as specified in the resolution.

linguistic_markers.function: Explicit self-identification of UNIDO as implementer of UNGA mandate — voluntary Policy Cascade

temporal_evidence.note: Extremely tight temporal coupling — UNIDO launch 3 days after resolution adoption, indicating pre-coordination

evidence_detail.confidence_note: CC-E (not CC-V) because evidence is from UNIDO leadership public statements at WAIC 2024, not from formal UNIDO programme documentation explicitly citing A/RES/78/311 as statutory/mandate authority. Elevation to CC-V would require formal UNIDO AIM programme documentation with explicit resolution citation.
evidence_detail.reclassification_note: Retyped from Type 6 Framework Family to Type 1 Policy Cascade (V5.0, February 2026). 'Operationalises mandate' = implementation relationship, not family membership. UNGA resolution (intergovernmental mandate) → UNIDO agency action (operational implementation).

sources.desc: A/RES/78/311 — Enhancing international cooperation on capacity-building of AI
UNIDO Press Release — WAIC 2024 / AIM Global CoE Launch

hierarchical_relationship.parent: UN General Assembly Resolution 78/311 — intergovernmental mandate establishing international cooperation framework for AI capacity-building in developing countries
hierarchical_relationship.child: UNIDO AIM Global Centre of Excellence (Shanghai) — UN specialised agency operationalising the mandate through technology transfer programmes to developing countries

connections.evidence_summary: Both comprehensive AI content regulations requiring labeling and transparency for AI-generated content; independent parallel development across Eastern/Western blocs

mechanism.evidence: Both binding regulations. China Deep Synthesis: CAC Departmental Rule (部门规章) with mandatory obligations. EU AI Act: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, directly applicable binding law.

target.evidence: Both target AI-generated content with labeling/transparency requirements. China Deep Synthesis Articles 16-17: mandatory labeling for AI-generated content, prohibition on removing labels. EU AI Act Article 50: transparency obligations requiring AI-generated content be marked in machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated, plus deepfake disclosure requirements.

scope.evidence: Both J-Tier A jurisdictions. China: ~$17.8T GDP. EU: ~$18.4T GDP. Both national/supranational binding frameworks with comparable regulatory reach.

timing.evidence: China Deep Synthesis effective: January 10, 2023. EU AI Act entry into force: August 1, 2024. Gap: 19 months. Outside 12-month Technology domain window.

evidence_detail.cross_bloc_note: This is a cross-bloc connection (T3 Eastern ↔ T3 Western). No evidence of bilateral coordination or mutual acknowledgment. Both represent independent sovereign approaches to AI content governance addressing similar technological challenges posed by generative AI and deepfakes.
evidence_detail.parallel_development_context: China's Deep Synthesis Provisions (2023) and EU AI Act (2024) both emerged from growing concerns about AI-generated disinformation and deepfakes. China's approach focuses on algorithm filing and platform responsibility; EU's approach emphasizes risk-based categorization with transparency obligations. Despite different regulatory philosophies, both converge on mandatory content labeling requirements.
evidence_detail.transparency_note: Cross-bloc Type 7 connections documented at CC-A confidence level. CC-A represents analytical assessment based on observable policy similarity, not documentary coordination.

evidence_detail.framework_relationship: DestinE (EU digital twin programme) + Leonardo (CINECA Italy pre-exascale) = EuroHPC JU compute infrastructure with dedicated allocation under Decision 30/2022

connections.evidence_summary: Both DestinE and MareNostrum 5 operate under EuroHPC JU; Decision 30/2022 allocates DestinE compute time on MareNostrum 5.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Destination Earth programme and MareNostrum 5 pre-exascale supercomputer (BSC, Spain) both designated under EuroHPC JU Council Regulation (EU) 2021/1173. DestinE is a Strategic Initiative; MareNostrum 5 is a pre-exascale hosting system.
evidence_detail.cross_references: EuroHPC JU Governing Board Decision 30/2022 allocates DestinE 5% of total EuroHPC computing time on LUMI, Leonardo, and MareNostrum 5.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: DestinE (EU digital twin programme) + MareNostrum 5 (BSC Spain pre-exascale) = EuroHPC JU compute infrastructure with dedicated allocation under Decision 30/2022

connections.evidence_summary: Both DestinE and JUPITER operate under EuroHPC JU Council Regulation (EU) 2021/1173; shared parent framework.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Destination Earth programme and JUPITER exascale supercomputer (Forschungszentrum Jülich, Germany) both designated under EuroHPC JU Council Regulation (EU) 2021/1173. DestinE is a Strategic Initiative; JUPITER is an exascale system. JUPITER not named in original DestinE allocation Decision 30/2022 but shares parent framework.
evidence_detail.cross_references: Council Regulation (EU) 2021/1173 establishes EuroHPC JU governance for both DestinE strategic initiatives and JUPITER exascale procurement.
evidence_detail.framework_relationship: DestinE (EU digital twin programme) + JUPITER (FZ Jülich Germany exascale) = EuroHPC JU framework members under shared parent regulation

connections.evidence_summary: China Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund Phase III (Big Fund III) is the sole limited partner of the National AI Industry Investment Fund, providing 100% of registered capital.

evidence_detail.primary_documentation: Big Fund III (¥344B semiconductor fund, E03) and the National AI Industry Investment Fund (¥60.06B AI fund), both Chinese state-backed investment vehicles registered in Shanghai.
evidence_detail.citation_text: The National AI Industry Investment Fund was registered in Shanghai on Jan. 19. It is a joint venture involving Guozhi Investment (Shanghai) Private Equity Fund Management, which is state-backed, and the China Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund Phase III, according to the business registry platform Qcc.com.
